rows.notes: Top-line title is fairly clear though old-style long-s forms and name spelling uncertain (I read 'Jacobi Edgecumbe'). No monetary columns on this line.

rows.description: adund die Novembris 1737 ad eundem Novemb: 1738
rows.notes: Second header line giving the date range. Handwriting legible; treated as header with no amounts.
Centered section label immediately under the title. The first word is somewhat uncertain (read as 'Averagia' or similar) but clearly a structural header; no amounts present.
Clear parish name 'Gwinear' and clearly aligned pounds/shillings/pence columns showing 60 00 00. Vertical ruling alignment unambiguous.

rows.description: Longwittenham pro dimid. anni: ad Mir. 1737
rows.notes: Description legible. Amount columns read as '30 02 11½' — pence shows a small loop interpreted as '½'. Alignment with vertical lines is clear; small risk in reading the final fraction but shape matches halfpenny.

rows.description: South Newington Reet. pro Bien: cum dim.
rows.notes: Parish name and 'Rect.' abbreviation legible. Pounds column looks like a three-digit number beginning with '1' and read as '149' — faintness of middle digit introduces ambiguity between 140/146/149; chosen as 149 based on stroke shapes and spacing in the pounds column.
Description and numbers clear. Pounds read '40', shillings and pence zeros clearly under ruling lines.
Clear reading: '20 00 00' aligned in pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Entry text and monetary figures '04 00 00' clearly visible; leading zero in pounds column apparent.

rows.description: Bigg alias Lord (now mr youd) pro Sequestranno
rows.notes: Description contains parenthetical remark that is faint but readable. Amounts read as '06 03 05½' — pence shows a small half-penny curl; slight ambiguity in reading some letters of the parenthesis but numeric alignment is clear.
Name appears as 'Calcot' or 'Calcol(t)' alias Lane; numbers read '03 03 00' with clear alignment. Slight uncertainty in spelling only.
Entry and figures read clearly as '09 18 02'. Shillings (18) within valid 0–19 range and pence 2 is unambiguous.

rows.description: Leadenhall al: Jesus Coll: pro Anno
rows.notes: Small amount, clearly '00 10 00' and description 'Jesus Coll.' legible. Alignment with columns unambiguous.
Clear reading of '01 00 00'. Description legible.
Amount '00 15 00' clear. Parenthetical alias legible; alignment with shilling column unambiguous.
Description likely 'Sadler' (handwriting slightly ornate). Amount reads '04 19 11' and pence is 11 (close to 12 but still valid). Chosen reading based on alignment and visible strokes.
Amount appears as '04 05 09½' — the pence shows a halfpenny curl. Description clear. Slight smudge near pence increases minor uncertainty.
Amount read as '00 06 10½' — pence contains a halfpenny mark. 'Hopkins' legible; abbreviation 'Mir.' and 'pro dim.' read from context.
Clear reading '04 14 09'. Description 'Clifton Ferry' unambiguous.
Description is a longer abbreviation; amount appears as '02 04 00'. There is a tiny mark after pence that could be a fractional mark but is faint; interpreted as none.
Entry and amount '06 00 00' clearly visible and within valid ranges.
Names are slightly abbreviated/contracted (read as 'Ledlane Sparks & Meriton'); amount reads as '17 13 04' and aligns well with the column rulings.
Parish likely 'Medenham' or similar; numbers read '06 13 04'. Slight uncertainty in parish spelling but numbers are clear.
Clear entry '04 00 00'. Name 'Boyce' legible.
Subtotal line at end of the first block. Number block boxed/ruled and clearly reads '372 09 09½'. Alignment with column lines clear; fraction is a halfpenny.
Centered heading starting the second block of receipts for the present year. No amounts on this line.
Likely 'E Thesauro' (to the Treasurer). Amount '50 00 00' is clear and aligned.
Similar to the earlier Longwittenham entry; amount reads '30 02 11½'. Text and fraction visible though small; interpreted as halfpenny.
Description 'Kidlington Vicar' legible and amount '04 00 00' clear.
Second instance of Lucas Eaton for the present year; amount clearly '02 00 00'.
Amount looks like '02 02 01½' — pence has a small curl interpreted as halfpenny. Parenthetical text faint but readable. Slight ambiguity in final pence digit due to ink density.

Institution line (appears to read 'Brunhouse Mr John's College' or 'Bushouse M Johns College'); amount '07 14 04' readable but the name is somewhat contracted leading to minor uncertainty in transcription.
Long amount '63 18 04' clearly in pounds/shillings/pence. 'Candlemass' spelled in old form; alignment with column rules good.
Repeat of Boyce entry for the present year; amount '04 00 00' clear.
Subtotal of the 'Recepta Anni presentis' block. Boxed/ruled figure reads as '163 17 09½'. Fraction appears as a halfpenny mark; alignment clean.
Section header (spelling uncertain: 'Battles' or 'Bates'?), no monetary values on the line. Description read from context; handwriting is somewhat ornate.

rows.description: Rec'd Mr Espert Invent at Mir. 1737
rows.notes: This line is faint and the description is hard to parse; amount appears as '79 06 02' with an additional small mark after pence that I interpret as three-quarters (0.75). The fraction mark is unclear (could be ½ or ¾); low confidence due to ink faintness and unusual fraction shape.
Short entry 'Ad Supper' and amount '02 00 00' clearly visible.
Section header introducing library/book entries; wording read as 'Commoners Library' but lettering is slightly flourished.

rows.description: for the Precept. in wh Mr Richards books were brought
rows.notes: Long descriptive line about books purchased; amount '02 10 00' aligned and readable. Some letters in description are faint; transcription follows the visible forms.

rows.description: from Mr Espart? Baran for xmas 1731 Mid. & xmas 1732
rows.notes: Description references purchases for Xmas years; proper names are contracted and uncertain ('Espert/Esparte/Esperd' and 'Baran/Barrow'), but amount '17 12 06' is clear and aligned.
A boxed/stacked total appears here reading roughly '101 06 08¾'. The pence fraction mark is faint and could be ¾; moderate to low confidence due to light ink and cramped digits.
Second subtotal in the stacked totals column reads '163 17 09½'. Matches the earlier subtotal for 'Recepta Anni presentis' and is boxed — digits and halfpenny mark are reasonably clear.
This repeats the earlier principal subtotal for the first block; boxed and very clear as '372 09 09½'. Alignment with ruling lines confirms column boundaries.

Final overall total at page bottom reads '637 16 03½' with a preceding abbreviated label 'Summ. Omn: receptor.' The digits are legible but fraction and some abbreviation strokes are small, so moderate confidence.
